overview: Dr. YSR ANU College of Engineering and Technology (Self-Finance) is a constituent college of Acharya Nagarjuna University (ANU), located in Guntur. Established in 2009, it offers technical education under a self-financing model, meaning its operations and infrastructure development are primarily funded through student tuition rather than direct government subsidies. It is a co-educational institution providing a range of modern engineering specializations.
accreditation: Approved by AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education). Affiliated with and governed by Acharya Nagarjuna University (ANU).

coursesAndFees.course: B.Tech (CSE, AI&ML, Data Science, Cyber Security, ECE, EEE, Civil, Mechanical)
coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics + AP EAPCET rank

coursesAndFees.course: M.Tech (Computer Science & Engineering, Digital Electronics & Communication)

admissions.admissionProcess: Admissions are conducted via state-level web counseling (APSCHE). 70% of seats are filled under the Convener Quota (Category A) based on AP EAPCET ranks (College Code: ANCUSF). The remaining 30% are Management Quota (Category B) seats filled based on merit or qualifying marks.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026: May 12–18, 2026; Registration (Late Fee): April 2026; Counseling: July 2026.

campus.area: Integrated within the Acharya Nagarjuna University campus
facilities: Separate Hostels for Boys (Ashoka, Satavahana) and Girls (Indira Priyadarshini)
Wi-Fi Connectivity across the Academic Block
campus.specialNote: Being part of a major state university, self-finance students have access to extensive university-wide resources, including advanced research facilities and multidisciplinary exposure. The fee structure is slightly higher than regular university seats but remains competitive compared to private engineering colleges.

contact.address: ANU Campus, Nambur, Guntur, Andhra Pradesh - 522510
